html:
Ramco Systems: the market is still valuing the scar tissue, not the inflection

Ramco is not the net-cash liquidation story in the older drafts. That was wrong. The latest reported cash balance was only USD 11.77 million at December 31, 2025, and FY25 consolidated cash plus current investments was roughly ₹73 crore. The real thesis is better but less lazy: this is a tiny, ignored Indian enterprise software product company whose operating model has finally crossed from “permanent R&D sink” to “vertical SaaS operating leverage.”

At ₹381.00, the market cap is about ₹1,427 crore on roughly 3.75 crore shares. After cash, enterprise value is roughly ₹1,325 crore. For that, you get a company running at about ₹700 crore FY26 revenue, with recurring revenue annualizing near USD 45 million, a USD 149.7 million unexecuted order book, and Q3 FY26 EBITDA margin of 26% despite a one-time labor-code charge below EBITDA. Q1, Q2, and Q3 FY26 revenue grew 15%, 18.5%, and about 14% year over year, respectively. The latest Q3 filing showed nine-month revenue from operations of ₹515.6 crore and profit before exceptional items of ₹48.3 crore versus a loss of ₹33.9 crore the prior year. That is not a melting ice cube.

The variant perception is that Ramco is being screened as a failed Indian software product story, while the useful parts are closer to specialized workflow infrastructure. Aviation MRO software is not generic ERP. It sits inside maintenance planning, component records, shop-floor execution, engineering compliance, supply chain, quality, billing, and regulator-sensitive records. Payroll is also not a toy category when it spans 150+ countries, statutory engines, Workday integration, and managed payroll workflows. The buyer is not choosing the cheapest app. The buyer is choosing the system it can trust when aircraft maintenance, defense readiness, or payroll compliance cannot break.

The aviation signal has become much stronger in the last three months. Korean Air went live with Ramco Aviation at its Engine Maintenance Center in April 2026, with more than 400 mechanics and engineers using Ramco’s mobile execution tools, and Ramco will support Korean Air’s 2027 engine maintenance cluster, described by the company as Asia’s largest engine MRO hub. Tata Advanced Systems selected Ramco in March 2026 for the digital foundation of its defense MRO facility supporting the Indian Air Force C-130J fleet. Sahar Group selected Ramco for UH-60 Black Hawk lifecycle support. ST Engineering signed an MoU with Ramco to explore next-generation aviation MRO technologies. These are not small reference logos for a company with a sub-₹1,500 crore market cap.

The payroll side is less exciting but could be more repeatable. Ramco Payce is now certified with Workday Global Payroll Connect, which matters because it lets Ramco ride the Workday ecosystem rather than compete directly with Workday HCM. Ramco says Payce is used by 500+ customers and covers 150+ countries. The current market is giving little value to that embedded channel option.

The stock’s recent weakness may be supply and memory, not fundamentals. In February 2026, promoter entity Ramco Management bought 10.2 lakh shares, or about 2.7% of the company, at about ₹500 per share for roughly ₹51 crore, while HDFC Mutual Fund sold nearly the same block. Promoters now hold about 55.7%, with no pledge disclosed. That means the float is tight, institutional ownership is thin, and the same block sale that created liquidity also likely capped the stock. Insider buying at ₹500 is not a guarantee, but it is a useful tell when the stock now trades 24% lower.

The accounting is the part investors must underwrite correctly. Ramco capitalizes a lot of product development and still carries ₹351 crore of consolidated intangibles. FY25 R&D expenditure was ₹76.7 crore, down from ₹82.8 crore, and intangible amortization plus impairment was heavy. This makes EBITDA look cleaner than free cash flow. The bull case requires revenue growth to keep compounding while R&D and implementation costs stop rising faster than revenue. The good news is that the first nine months of FY26 show exactly that direction: revenue is up, EBITDA is expanding, normalized PBT has turned positive, recurring revenue is stable, and the order book remains large.

The market is paying roughly 1.8 to 1.9x FY26 run-rate revenue and about 3.3x annualized recurring revenue. That is cheap if Ramco is now a 12% to 18% grower with improving cash conversion and sticky vertical products. It is expensive if the company keeps capitalizing R&D forever just to stand still. The next proof point is the audited FY26 result, expected around late May 2026. The line items that matter are Q4 order bookings, cash balance, capitalized development spend, recurring revenue, and whether normalized PBT continues to rise.

The best version of the thesis is simple: Ramco spent too long looking like a product company that could not monetize its engineering. Now, at exactly the moment when aviation MRO digitization, defense localization, Workday-connected payroll, and AI-enabled workflow automation are becoming more relevant, the company is showing operating leverage and landing reference customers that are too large for the current valuation to ignore.

Bear case: ₹250, down 34%. Order bookings remain stuck around USD 10 million per quarter, Q4 disappoints, capitalized development stays high, and the market values Ramco as a low-quality services-heavy software company at roughly 1.0x forward sales.

Base case: ₹650, up 71%. FY27 revenue reaches ₹800 crore to ₹850 crore, EBITDA holds in the low-to-mid 20s, free cash flow becomes visibly positive after product capex, and the market rerates the company to around 2.7x forward sales or a mid-teens multiple of normalized FY27 operating earnings.

Bull case: ₹1,050, up 176%. Korean Air, TASL, ST Engineering, and new engine MRO wins prove Ramco Aviation is a scarce vertical SaaS asset, Payce scales through Workday-connected payroll, recurring revenue moves above 60%, and the company is valued closer to 4x forward revenue or 5x to 6x recurring revenue. Current stock price: ₹381.00.
